creator: hrs@FreeBSD.org
text:
----Security_Multipart(Fri_Jan_13_14_58_00_2012_557)--
Content-Type: Text/Plain; charset=us-ascii
Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit

Garrett Cooper <yaneurabeya@gmail.com> wrote
  in <201109212342.p8LNg4jo010529@red.freebsd.org>:

gc> If you load the vboxnet module or service at boot, the vboxnet0 interface becomes available:
gc>
gc> $ ifconfig vboxnet0
gc> vboxnet0: flags=8802<BROADCAST,SIMPLEX,MULTICAST> metric 0 mtu 1500
gc>         ether 0a:00:27:00:00:00
gc>         nd6 options=29<PERFORMNUD,IFDISABLED,AUTO_LINKLOCAL>
gc>
gc> This interface shouldn't be configured with IPv6.
gc>
gc> This patch also sorts the interface list.

 I am not sure if adding it into noafif() is a good idea because
 AFAICC vboxnet0 interface can be used with IPv6 when it is configured
 with the host-only network mode.  It is almost always configured by
 VirtualBox process, but there should be no bad side effect even if it
 is not in noafif().  Do you have any specific problem with it?

-- Hiroki
